tamil-nadu_tiruppur.production_scale: ₹50,000 crore total business in FY19 (₹26,000 crore exports + ₹24,000 crore domestic sales)
tamil-nadu_tiruppur.export_value: ₹26,000 crore in FY19 (8.3% growth compared to ₹24,000 crore in previous fiscal)
tamil-nadu_tiruppur.key_facts: Tiruppur Exporters' Association (TEA) is India's leading readymade/knitwear cluster | Kids and women garments drive exports, garnering 70% of total businesses | Last six months of FY19 showed 31.15% average export growth over corresponding period in 2017-18 | National readymade garment exports recorded 4.7% growth to ₹1,12,715 crore in FY 2018-19 | Cluster targeted ₹30,000 crore in both exports and domestic sales for FY 2019-20, aiming for total business of ₹60,000 crore | Ambitious target of ₹100,000 crore by fiscal 2022

uttar-pradesh_varanasi.product_varieties: Benarasi sari, handwoven sarees, silk sarees, cotton sarees

andhra-pradesh_anantapuram-anantapur.unit_count: 20 units with 100-150 employees each, plus around 400 families involved in home-based stitching
andhra-pradesh_anantapuram-anantapur.product_varieties: stitched jeans of various brands and designs, Heavy distressed shaded washed light grey Jeans, cotton Lycra fabric with stretch
andhra-pradesh_anantapuram-anantapur.export_countries: other parts of the country, Hyderabad, Bangalore, Chennai
andhra-pradesh_anantapuram-anantapur.raw_materials: raw jeans cloth from Ahmadabad and Mumbai, zips, buttons, threads, lining cloth for inner pockets from Bangalore and Bellary of Karnataka District
andhra-pradesh_anantapuram-anantapur.key_facts: Nearly every household in Rayadurg is involved in some aspect of jeans manufacturing | Women comprise 60-70 percent of the total workforce | Jeans production is the second largest employment avenue after agriculture | Products supplied to branded jeans companies and malls in Hyderabad, Bangalore, Chennai | Production process includes cutting, stitching, dyeing, ironing, and packing | Rayadurg has gained recognition for expertise in jeans stitching attracting orders from prominent companies

andhra-pradesh_ananthpuram-anantapur.unit_count: 20 units with 100-150 employees each | approximately 400 families involved in home-based stitching
andhra-pradesh_ananthpuram-anantapur.product_varieties: stitched jeans of various brands and designs | heavy distressed shaded washed light grey jeans | cotton Lycra fabric with stretch
andhra-pradesh_ananthpuram-anantapur.export_countries: exported to other parts of the country | supplied to Hyderabad, Bangalore, Chennai
andhra-pradesh_ananthpuram-anantapur.raw_materials: raw jeans cloth from Ahmadabad and Mumbai | zips, buttons, threads, lining cloth from Bangalore and Bellary of Karnataka District
andhra-pradesh_ananthpuram-anantapur.key_facts: Approximately 60,000 jeans manufactured per day in Rayadurg | Women comprise 60-70 percent of workforce | Nearly every household involved in jeans manufacturing | Process includes cutting, stitching, dyeing, ironing, packing, and washing | Jeans production is second largest employment avenue after agriculture

andhra-pradesh_annamayya-rayachoty.employment_total: more than 10 crore people (direct and indirect employment in cotton textile industry)
andhra-pradesh_annamayya-rayachoty.key_facts: India got 1st place in world cotton acreage with 114.47 lakh hectares (36% of world's 314.79 lakh hectares) | India's cotton yield stands at 437 kg lint/ha compared to world average of 776 kg lint/ha | Approximately 67% of Indian cotton is produced on rain-fed areas and 33% on irrigated lands | Cotton production for 2024-25 estimated at 294.5 lakh bales (170 kg each), 9.5% lower than 2023-24 | Cotton consumption estimated at 318 lakh bales in 2024-25 | MSP for Medium Staple cotton in 2025-26 fixed at ₹7710 | MSP for Long Staple cotton in 2025-26 fixed at ₹8110 | Cotton production in 2023-24 was 325.22 lakh bales | Procurement under MSP in 2024-25 was 100.16 lakh bales (34.04% of production)

andhra-pradesh_bapatla.unit_count: 566 MDL Units manufacture Granite slabs; 23 units are exporting

andhra-pradesh_bapatla.scheme_names: Slab System for voluntary Seigniorage Fee collection
andhra-pradesh_bapatla.key_facts: Black Granite found in Gurijepalli; Colour Granite found in Konidena | Export-oriented units located in Maddipadu, Annangi, and Gundlapalli villages of Prakasam district | Bapatla granites showcased at STONA and Jaipur Expo | Currently using 7 mm wires, aiming to adopt 1 mm precision wires | Equipment includes wire saws, auto polishers, multi-cutting blades (21 blades), edge cutters, and auto polishing machines (24 heads)

andhra-pradesh_chittoor.daily_output: Rs 2 to Rs 3 lakh business a day (Patur traders)
andhra-pradesh_chittoor.product_varieties: silk sarees, cotton sarees with minimal design
andhra-pradesh_chittoor.success_story_detail: Traders expecting Rs 30 crore business this season | Patur traders reporting Rs 2 to Rs 3 lakh business a day
andhra-pradesh_chittoor.key_facts: Venkatagiri and Patur in Nellore district are famous for handloom sarees | Traders receiving online orders from other parts of the country | Sarees sourced from Madanapalle, Thamballapalle, Nagari, Puttur, Srikalahasti, Kalakada and parts of Chittoor district | Business provided respite after Covid-19 pandemic | Peak demand during Sravana Masam for weddings and Varalakshmi Vratam

andhra-pradesh_dr-br-ambedkar-konaseema.key_facts: Global banana fiber market size was valued at USD 104.49 Billion in 2025 | Market expected to reach USD 173.56 Billion by 2034 | Market exhibiting a CAGR of 5.8% from 2026 to 2034 | Banana fiber consists of natural lignocellulosic fibers | Fibers are processed through mechanical and chemical treatments | Fibers are strong, lightweight, and biodegradable

andhra-pradesh_east-godavari.employment_total: 300 persons in weaving and allied activities
andhra-pradesh_east-godavari.product_varieties: Cotton Shirting, Cotton Sarees, Dhoties, Towels, Malkha Sarees, Malkha cloth
andhra-pradesh_east-godavari.success_story_detail: Provides employment to around 300 persons in weaving and allied activities | Operates own Yarn Mill and Dyeing unit (organic and chemical dyeing) | Retail shop at Dwarapudi | Provides old age pension to members and education support to their children
andhra-pradesh_east-godavari.key_facts: Malkha cloth uses sustainable and eco-friendly production process | Pulugurtha Handloom Weavers Cooperative Society established on principles of cooperation, self-help, and mutual assistance | Products marketed through exhibitions, trade fairs, online platforms, and tie-ups with retailers | Uniqueness: Fiber to Fabric production from own Yarn Mill through own Dyeing unit | Malkha and Pulugurtha Cotton Fabric identified as One District One Product for East Godavari District

andhra-pradesh_eluru.key_facts: A Coffee Table book on ODOP Handicraft and Handloom was created and published | ODOP Pavilion was set up in premier summits such as Global Investors Summit and G20 Summits with live craft making demos, artisan interaction, and product showcase

andhra-pradesh_guntur.product_varieties: Armour, US-341, BSS-355, Gayathri-155, Byadagi, Classic, Teja, LCA-341, LCA-625, LCA-620, LCH-111, LCA-657, LCA-680, 2222, 5531, Indam No.5, Tejeswini, 250, 275
andhra-pradesh_guntur.export_countries: China, Vietnam, Thailand, Sri Lanka, Indonesia, Malaysia
andhra-pradesh_guntur.gi_info: Guntur Sannam Chilli registered with Geographical Indications (GI) registry of Government of India in 2009
andhra-pradesh_guntur.key_facts: Guntur district cultivates chilli in 18,400 ha | Chilli occupies 75% of total Horticultural area in the district | Guntur Market Yard is the largest chilli market in Asia | Guntur district produces 15% of chillies area produced in India | Andhra Pradesh contributes 40% of India's chilli production | Andhra Pradesh produces 14.43 lakh tonnes of dry chilli covered under 2.46 lakh ha which contributes 32% of India's chilli production | India produces 1.98 million tonnes and contributes 43% of world chilli production | Chilli contributes 42% of total spice export quantity of the country | Chilli is known to Indians for about 400 years | 60% of chilli cultivation area is covered with Hybrid varieties and 40% with open pollinated varieties | Capsicum annum is the botanical name | Solanaceae is the family | Mexico is the origin

andhra-pradesh_kakinada.gi_info: GI Registration on 10.04.2008 under Geographical Registry, Government of India
andhra-pradesh_kakinada.key_facts: Uppada Jamdani Sarees are diaphanous silk sarees originating from Uppada in Andhra Pradesh | Jamdani is a hand-woven fabric also known as muslin | Two weavers are required to sit in a pit loom and work in tandem to make this fabric | Design blends into the cloth and is clearly visible and distinguishable on both sides | On the reverse there are no loose unattended ends | Design cannot be felt separately on the surface of the cloth | Weaving techniques use Galla, Adda and numerous thilis depending on the design | The word Jamdani roughly translates to flower vase (jam = flower, dani = vase) with Persian origin | The technique of weaving has Bengali roots | ODOP initiative is aimed at fostering balanced regional development across all districts of the country

andhra-pradesh_krishna.product_varieties: block print sarees, rugs, hand towels, paintings, door covers, bed covers, clothing material
andhra-pradesh_krishna.raw_materials: vegetable dyes, plants, barks, seeds, flowers, leaves, roots, cotton cloth, wooden blocks
andhra-pradesh_krishna.key_facts: Pedana is 13 km from Machilipatnam | Machilipatnam/Pedana Kalamkari is one of two Kalamkari styles in India (other being Srikalahasti style) | Production geographically limited to Pedana town and neighboring villages | Word Kalamkari comes from Persian word 'kalam' (pen) and 'Kari' (craftsmanship) | Style evolved during Mughal Dynasty rule and practiced by Golconda Sultanate | Wall hanger from 15th Century AD displayed in Victoria Museum, London

andhra-pradesh_kurnool.raw_materials: Iron ore, Dolomite, Lime stone, Ochre, Quartz, Steatite, Silica
andhra-pradesh_kurnool.key_facts: Kurnool district lies in Rayalseema region of Andhra Pradesh | Average rainfall is 670mm | Geographical area is 1765800 hectares | District is bounded by Rivers Tungabhadra and Krishna on the north | Nallamala and Erramalas are two important mountain ranges running parallel from north to south | 18% of total geographical area is under forest cover | Iron ore production 2010-11: 708096 tonnes | Dolomite production 2010-11: 562377 tonnes | Lime stone production 2010-11: 9965709 tonnes | District has 3 revenue divisions: Kurnool, Nandyal and Adoni | 898 Gram Panchayats in the district

andhra-pradesh_nandyal.product_varieties: Plain towels, Plain lungies, white colour, chengai colour
andhra-pradesh_nandyal.key_facts: Origin of Handloom towels and lungies traced back to about 100 years ago | Woven in Nandyal district | Made of cotton, highly heat absorbent | Nandivargam area famous for weaving coarse variety | Production techniques include Warp Preparation, Weft Insertion, Bleaching, Dyeing, Sizing warping

andhra-pradesh_ntr-vijaywada.product_varieties: Dasavatharams, Lord Seta Ram Laxman, Lord Vishnu, Lord Brahma, agricultural activities scenes, women grinding flour, men ascending palm trees
andhra-pradesh_ntr-vijaywada.raw_materials: white Poniki wood (Tella Poniki wood), ankudu wood, vegetable dyes, enamel paints
andhra-pradesh_ntr-vijaywada.gi_info: Geographical Indication (GI) status granted in 2006 under the Ministry of Culture, Govt of India
andhra-pradesh_ntr-vijaywada.key_facts: Kondapalli toys have origins dating back nearly four centuries | Kondapalli village is situated 25 kilometers from Vijayawada | Kondapalli artisans are known as Arya Kshatriyas | King Sri Krishna Devaraya provided patronage to the craft | White Poniki wood is sourced from the vicinity of Ibrahimpatnam | Toys are known for vibrant colors, lightweight wood, and intricate details | Challenges include scarcity of raw material (tella poniki and ankudu wood), fluctuating prices, stagnant product prices, and market competition

andhra-pradesh_palnadu-narasaraopeta.product_varieties: idols of gods and goddesses, Buddha in meditative posture, man and woman in love, miniature carvings of animals and nature
andhra-pradesh_palnadu-narasaraopeta.success_story_detail: master sculptor of Durgi stone craft continuing to create stone carvings
andhra-pradesh_palnadu-narasaraopeta.raw_materials: soft limestone called Suddha Rai, whitish gray stone
andhra-pradesh_palnadu-narasaraopeta.key_facts: Durgi stone carvings date back to 3rd century AD | craftsmen belong to Viswa Brahmins community classified as BCs in A.P. | limestone is quarried using machines and found under earth's surface | style drew inspiration from Amaravathi school of art | linked to earliest Palaeolithic civilisations in lower Krishna basin | decline mentioned due to lack of access to organised market and govt. support, declining demand

andhra-pradesh_parvathipuram-manyam.key_facts: District area: 3659 sq km | Population: 9.25 lakhs | Literacy rate: 50.9% | Revenue divisions: 2 | Mandals: 15 | Villages: 967 | Municipality: 2 | Nagar Panchayat: 1

andhra-pradesh_prakasam.product_varieties: Rough Blocks, Gangsaw Slabs, Cutterslabs, Countertops, Granite Tiles, Monuments, Steps, Treads, Pool Copings, Window Sills, Thresholds, Black Galaxy Granite, Kashmir White Granite, Red Multicolor Granite, Tan Brown Granite
andhra-pradesh_prakasam.export_countries: France, China, Japan, United States, United Kingdom, Germany, UAE, Europe, Australia
andhra-pradesh_prakasam.key_facts: India leads the world in granite exports | India accounts for 15% of global granite production | 1,283 Indian exporters sold to 3,245 customers in 2023-24 | Over 20% of granite exports from India go to the United States | Anticipated 4.1% annual increase in granite demand until 2023 | Top importers are USA, UK, Germany, China, and Japan | Top three HSN codes: 68022390, 68022310, 25161100

andhra-pradesh_spsr-nellore.key_facts: Udayagiri Wooden Cutlery is hand-carved | Products are from Udayagiri, Andhra Pradesh | The town is renowned for practicing wooden cutlery craftsmanship | Product prices range from ₹270 to ₹460 | 11 products in stock and 5 out of stock

andhra-pradesh_sri-sathya-sai-puttaparth.production_scale: 26,242.6 MT cocoon production (3,619.6 MT raw silk equivalent) in FY 2024-25
andhra-pradesh_sri-sathya-sai-puttaparth.employment_total: 7.85 million employed in sericulture industry in India across rural and semi-urban areas
andhra-pradesh_sri-sathya-sai-puttaparth.key_facts: Sri Sathya Sai district holds top position in silk production within Andhra Pradesh | In FY 2024-25 the district recorded 56,110 acres under mulberry | 29,097 sericulture farmers in Sri Sathya Sai district (SES 2024-25) | Andhra Pradesh ranks as second-largest producer of silk after Karnataka | India is second-largest producer of silk after China | Sri Sathya Sai district is in Rayalaseema region with regulated cocoon markets

andhra-pradesh_srikakulam.key_facts: Cashew is cultivated in around 24,000 hectares in the district | The extent of bearing orchards is 22,000 hectares | Cashew is cultivated in all geographical agronomical conditions viz., Plains, Tribal high altitude zones, and Sea Coasts | Cashew is the main livelihood horticulture crop in Tekkali Jurisdiction and the district in general | Access to processing industries is available

andhra-pradesh_tirupati.product_varieties: cotton sarees, silk mix sarees, pure silk sarees
andhra-pradesh_tirupati.key_facts: Venkatagiri sarees are weaved on traditional pit looms | Art of Jamdani weaving followed from about 14 generations | Weaving started during the time of Venkatagiri Raja | Previously Padmashali Devanga communities were mostly involved in this work | Craftsmen from five generations are involved in making Venkatagiri sarees | Pure zari threads are brought from Kanchipuram | Venkatagiri is a small village of Tirupati district

andhra-pradesh_visakhapatnam.product_varieties: Frozen shrimp, Frozen fish, Frozen squid
andhra-pradesh_visakhapatnam.export_countries: United States, China, European Union, Southeast Asia, Japan, Middle East
andhra-pradesh_visakhapatnam.export_value: $2.19 billion (29.72% of India's $7.38 billion total seafood exports in FY24) | $7.45 billion (India's total seafood exports in FY25)
andhra-pradesh_visakhapatnam.key_facts: Visakhapatnam Port handled $2.19 billion out of India's $7.38 billion seafood exports in FY24 | Frozen shrimp accounted for $5.17 billion (69.46% of export earnings and 43.67% by volume) in FY25 | United States imported $2.71 billion worth of Indian seafood | Export volumes fell from 17,81,602 tonnes in FY24 to 16,98,170 tonnes in FY25 | Visakha Container Terminal has 6 lakh TEUs capacity and can accommodate three vessels simultaneously | Container terminal equipped with 650 reefer power sockets with 24-hour generator backup | Real-time temperature monitoring system triggers alerts if deviation exceeds 5°C from set point | Port examination point has 24 reefer sockets with own backup power supply

andhra-pradesh_vizianagaram.key_facts: Vizianagaram District area: 4124 sq km | Population: 19.30 lacks | Literacy Rate: 53.21% | Revenue Divisions: 3 | Mandals: 27 | Gram Panchayats: 777 | Villages: 982 | Municipal Corporation: 1

andhra-pradesh_ysr-kadapa.product_varieties: Barium sulphate, Barium Chloride, Barium Carbonate
andhra-pradesh_ysr-kadapa.key_facts: YSR District is divided into 4 Revenue divisions with 36 Mandals | District has 1 Municipal Corporation and 6 Municipalities | Major Minerals in the district are Barytes, Limestone, and Asbestos | Minor Minerals include Napa Slabs, Road Metal, Building Stone, Marble, Mosaic Chips and Rehmatti | Asbestos of Chrysotile variety occurs at contact of Vempalle dolamite with dolerite in Pulivendula taluk | Barytes Barium Compounds were selected under ODOP initiative by District Industries Export Promotion Committee (DIEPC) | Major exportable products are Barium Chemicals

arunachal-pradesh_anjaw.production_scale: 150 metric tonnes with financial turnover of Rs nearly 12 crore during financial year 2010-11
arunachal-pradesh_anjaw.cluster_names: T'Pani, Metalliang, Bomna, Methumna in Chaglagam circle, Watong, Hawai
arunachal-pradesh_anjaw.success_story_detail: Farmers with small holdings earn a minimum of Rs 3 to 4 lakh, and a maximum of Rs 18 lakh annually
arunachal-pradesh_anjaw.key_facts: Anjaw district was once an opium producer | 2,300 hectares of land brought under large cardamom cultivation | Crop loss detected due to fungal infection in certain areas | Vector-borne disease called 'Chirke' found in Watong and Hawai areas | Scientists from ICAR visited and suggested proper sanitation, nutrient application, and irrigation | Every house in the hilly belt has cardamom plantation

arunachal-pradesh_changlang.export_countries: Gulf Cooperation Council destinations, European Union, Japan
arunachal-pradesh_changlang.key_facts: Areca nut market size valued at USD 1.19 billion in 2025 | Estimated to grow from USD 1.25 billion in 2026 to USD 1.57 billion by 2031 at 4.66% CAGR | Asia-Pacific accounted for about 88% of market size in 2025 | Africa is fastest-growing region at 6.1% CAGR from 2026 to 2031 | India's October 2025 extension includes Minimum Import Price and 100% basic customs duty | Yellow Leaf Disease outbreak in Karnataka raises production risk | Premium pricing available for pesticide-residue-certified shipments | Enduring cultural chewing habits in South and Southeast Asia impact +0.8% on CAGR | Expansion of domestic processing capacity in India and Indonesia impacts +0.6% on CAGR

arunachal-pradesh_dibang-valley.scheme_names: Deen Dayal Swavalamban Yojna, Deen Dayal Upadhyay Bunkar Yojna, Chief Minister Krishi Rinn Yojna
arunachal-pradesh_dibang-valley.key_facts: Dibang Valley District came into existence on 1st June 1980 with headquarter at Anini | District area: 9,142 Sq Km | Population: 8,004 | Literacy Rate: 64.1% | 3 blocks and 142 villages | Language: Idu Mishmi (Dialect) | Festivals: Reh & Keh-Meh-Haa

arunachal-pradesh_east-kameng.key_facts: East Kameng district is situated in the western part of Arunachal Pradesh | The name 'Kameng' is derived from the Kameng river, a tributary of the Brahmaputra | The district has 3 Sub-Divisions: Seppa, Bameng, and Chayang Tajo | The district has 12 Circles | Principal tribes are Nyishi, Aka, Miji, and Puroik (Sollung) | The district has 1 Parliamentary Constituency (Arunachal West) and 4 Assembly constituencies | Altitude varies from 363 meters to 1906 meters | October to March are most favorable months for development work | There are 2 Police stations at Seppa and Chayantajo and 2 outposts at Bameng and Khenewa

arunachal-pradesh_east-siang.product_varieties: Solid Jaggery, Liquid Jaggery, Powdered Jaggery
arunachal-pradesh_east-siang.raw_materials: sugarcane juice, date palm sap, coconut sap
arunachal-pradesh_east-siang.key_facts: Global jaggery market valued at $4.8 billion in 2025 | Expected to reach $8.6 billion by 2034 at CAGR of 6.7% | Solid jaggery held largest product type share at 54.2% in 2025 | Food and Beverages segment dominated with 61.8% revenue share | Asia Pacific led with $2.6 billion revenue, 54.4% global share | Sugarcane accounted for 72.3% of market share | India accounts for over 70% of global jaggery production | Report spans 273+ pages | Shree Renuka Sugars Ltd. and EID Parry (India) Ltd. are leading companies | Jaggery contains minerals: iron, magnesium, potassium, phosphorus, antioxidants, and traces of vitamins

arunachal-pradesh_kamle.cluster_names: Wakro-Lohit, Roing-Dambuk-Lower Dibang Valley, Pangin, Mebo-East Siang, Boleng-Upper Siang, Basar-West Siang, Boha, Bragan-West Kameng, Bana-East Kameng
arunachal-pradesh_kamle.scheme_names: PM Micro Food Processing Enterprises Upgrading Scheme (Pradhan Mantri Sukshm Khad Udyog Unnayan Yojana), Atma Nirbhar Bharat Abhiyaan
arunachal-pradesh_kamle.key_facts: Mandarin Orange is the oldest cultivated major fruit of Arunachal Pradesh | Citrus accounts for approximately 90 percent of the total orange population in Arunachal Pradesh | Fruit crops cover more than 45 percent of total area and contribute approximately 57 percent to total fruit production in the state | One tree produces an average of 200 to 300 fruits per year | Peak harvest season is November-February | Arunachal oranges have high juice content with lowest acidity among Indian varieties | Oranges are grown organically using indigenous pest and disease control methods | Commercial popularity began after the 1970s when government established schemes to encourage citrus cultivation

arunachal-pradesh_kra-daadi.success_story_detail: Partnered to launch limited-edition Fuji Apple and Cardamom sparkling drink in 2024
arunachal-pradesh_kra-daadi.export_countries: Guatemala, India, Saudi Arabia, Europe, North America, Middle East
arunachal-pradesh_kra-daadi.export_value: Market size USD 1.73 Billion (2026), USD 2.46 Billion (2031)
arunachal-pradesh_kra-daadi.key_facts: Cardamom market valued at USD 1.62 billion in 2025 | Asia-Pacific region captured 38.3% of cardamom market share in 2025 | Middle East is fastest-growing region with 8.3% CAGR through 2031 | Market forecast growth rate 7.30% CAGR (2026-2031) | Saudi Arabia import prices almost doubled in 2024 ahead of Ramadan-to-Hajj peaks | Smallholders in Kerala and Karnataka shifting acreage toward turmeric and ginger | Blockchain-verified lots winning price premiums in Europe and North America

arunachal-pradesh_kurung-kumey.product_varieties: Dzongu Golsey, Sawney, Seremna, Ramsey, Varlangey
arunachal-pradesh_kurung-kumey.key_facts: Large cardamom (Amomum subulatum Roxb.) is the third most expensive spice after saffron and vanilla | Study evaluated 41 large cardamom cultivation sites in Sikkim with elevations ranging between 975 and 2069 m asl | Five cultivars were studied: Dzongu Golsey, Sawney, Seremna, Ramsey, and Varlangey | Yield-related traits measured include number of capsules per spike, capsule length, capsule width, fresh and dry capsule weight, number of seeds per locule, fresh and dry seed weight | Low-elevation cultivars Seremna and Dzongu Golsey are suitable at elevation 975 m | Mid-elevation range is 975–1515 m | High-elevation cultivars like Ramsey are suitable at elevation 1515 m or above | Dzongu Golsey, Sawney, and Seremna showed better yield at mid-elevation (975–1515 m) than at high-elevation | Ramsey and Varlangey performed better at high elevation (≥1515 m) than at mid-elevation | Large cardamom is native to eastern Himalayan region and was domesticated first by indigenous Lepcha tribe of Sikkim

arunachal-pradesh_leparada.product_varieties: different varieties, pungency, size, shape and colours
arunachal-pradesh_leparada.gi_info: Geographical Indication (GI) registered by Nagaland State Government in 2006 with 1,001,304 Scoville heat units (SHU)
arunachal-pradesh_leparada.key_facts: Study conducted during 2019-2021 with 60 King chilli respondents | Overall benefit-cost ratio was 3.27:1 | Four marketing channels were identified | Marketing efficiency by Acharya's method was highest (0.13) on channel A | King chilli is grown commercially in Nagaland for its fruits as principal source of dry chillies | Used for medicinal purposes, ingredients, pickles, and extraction of oleoresin and capsaicin | Capsicin content of Capsicum chinense found to be very high compared to other chilli species | King chilli is used in bulk quantities in fresh and dried forms

arunachal-pradesh_lohit.key_facts: Global mustard seed market valued at USD 804.81 million in 2025, projected to reach USD 1.14 billion by 2034 | CAGR of 3.96% during forecast period | Nepal is largest producer contributing 27% of global supply as of 2022 | Russia contributes 27.28% and Canada 19.69% | Mustard seeds contain 26.08g protein per 100g, 36.24g healthy fats, and 30-36% oil | Contains calcium 266mg, magnesium 370mg, iron 9.21mg per 100g | Asia-Pacific held largest market share in 2025 with India leading | Europe fastest-growing region with CAGR of 3.68% | Industrial applications dominated with 45.9% market share in 2025 | Indirect sales channels accounted for 61.6% of market

assam_west-karbi-anglong.key_facts: 2,600 hectare highland area used for organic ginger cultivation | Karbi Anglong ginger is medium-size rhizome with yellow skin and distinctive pungency | Shelf-life of 9 months | Two types of ginger grown: Nadia (fibrous, for domestic use) and Aizol (less fibre, for export) | Cultivation traced back to early 1950s | Sowing in March-April, harvest in January | Organic cultivation method with no chemicals or fertilizers | Three-fourth of total ginger production of Assam state from two hill districts (Karbi Anglong and Dima Hasao) | In 2009, 1,000 heavy trucks carried ginger from these areas to New Delhi; by 2021, only 200 trucks | In 2009, government spent Rs 35 lakh for ginger processing unit

bihar_madhubani.product_varieties: paintings on paper, paintings on clothes, wall paintings

bihar_madhubani.key_facts: Madhubani paintings are also known as Mithila paintings | The art originated in villages of Jitwarpur and Ranti near the city of Madhubani | The word Madhubani means 'honey forest' | King Janak of Mithila Kingdom (8th or 7th century BCE) is believed to have commissioned these paintings for his daughter Sita's wedding | Central themes are love and fertility | Traditionally made by women of the Mithila region | Knowledge passed from mothers to daughters through generations | Characterized by eye-catching geometrical patterns | Traditional themes include worship of Hindu deities (Radha and Krishna, Rama, Shiva, Ganesha, Laxmi, Saraswati), nature, mythology, wedding scenes and social events | Paintings traditionally done on plastered walls or mud walls, now also on paper and clothes due to commercial demand
bihar_madhubani._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

bihar_munger.product_varieties: Lemon Grass (Cymbopogon citratus), also known as Tanglad

bihar_munger.export_countries: Canada, Australia, Cyprus, Estonia, Hong Kong, Oman, Philippines, Russia, Singapore, Switzerland, Taiwan, Thailand, Turkey, Ukraine, Vietnam
bihar_munger.key_facts: Lemongrass is a perennial plant native to the Philippines | Leaves grow up to one meter in length and are flat in shape | Plant requires warm and humid climate with rainfall ranging from 250-300 cm uniformly distributed | Grows well at altitudes between 100-1,200 m above sea level | Seeds are collected January to February after flowering in November-December | Healthy plant gives about 100-200 grams of seeds | About 10 kilograms of seeds are adequate to raise seedlings for planting 1 hectare | Seedlings are ready for transplanting when about 60 days old | Seedlings planted at distance of 45-60 cm in rows 60-70 cm apart | Lemongrass oil has anti-fungal properties | Commonly used in teas, soups, and curries
bihar_munger._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

chhattisgarh_korba.key_facts: The mahua tree is revered by tribal communities across northern Maharashtra, eastern Rajasthan, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, southern Bihar, northern Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, West Bengal and Odisha | An estimated 90 percent of mahua production goes into making alcohol | Tribal families are not allowed to store more than five litres of mahua liquor | The road between Ambikapur and Ranchi sees over 300 truckloads of mahua cross every year | By May 21, 2018, around 26,000 tonnes of mahua flowers had arrived at Agricultural Produce Marketing Committees, with Chhattisgarh contributing 55 percent | Ranchi (Jharkhand) is the biggest mahua market that determines price across India | For over 75 lakh Adivasis across central and eastern India, mahua is a major cash crop | Mahua is among the top five minor forest produce (MFP) in India | In Chhattisgarh and Madhya Pradesh, mahua is the biggest MFP by revenue

himachal-pradesh_bilaspur.daily_output: 1 ton/hr pulp in season (130 days/4 months)
himachal-pradesh_bilaspur.product_varieties: chayavanprash, ayurvedic medicines (TRIFALA), cosmetic products (hair oils, creams), preserve, juice, ready-to-serve drink, sherbet, jam, fruit bar, dehydrated amla whole or powder, mouth fresheners
himachal-pradesh_bilaspur.key_facts: Amla (Phyllanthus emblica) is Indian gooseberry with ascorbic acid content varying from 0.9% to 1.3%, the second highest among all cultivated fruits | Proposed project processes amla at 2.5 tons/hr to produce 1 ton/hr pulp in 130-day season (4 months) | Pulp can be used directly or stored for processing into value-added products during remaining 170 days of year | Amla is a cornerstone in traditional Ayurvedic medicine, credited with bolstering immune system, enhancing liver function, promoting hair growth, improving eyesight, and facilitating bodily rejuvenation | Global demand for herbal and holistic health solutions has increased, with consumers preferring natural alternatives to artificial dietary supplements
himachal-pradesh_bilaspur._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

himachal-pradesh_chamba.key_facts: Chamba Rumal and Chamba Chappal are items of national fame | Chamba district lies in the Himalayas and partially fringes the Shivalik range of hills | Chamba district is one of the largest districts of Himachal Pradesh with an area of 6528 square kilometers spread over 1144 inhabited villages | A majority of rural households are engaged in traditional vocations such as handloom weaving, wood and metal works, leather foot wear, rearing of goats and sheep | Handloom industry in HP is providing gainful employment to about 45000 handloom weavers | Chamba Rumal is one of the district-specific ODOP products from Himachal Pradesh

himachal-pradesh_hamirpur.key_facts: Content includes Uttar Pradesh Hamirpur district government page (not Himachal Pradesh) | Himachal Pradesh Agriculture Department page contains crop production data for HP state (maize, paddy, wheat, barley, pulses, oil seeds) but does not specify Hamirpur district ODOP details | Maharashtra MAITRI page discusses agro-food processing sector nationally but does not contain Hamirpur-specific information
himachal-pradesh_hamirpur._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article

himachal-pradesh_kangra.success_story_detail: owner of Himalayan Brew Tea company, independently reclaimed 150 hectare land in recent years
himachal-pradesh_kangra.export_countries: Europe, Central Asia, Australia, Afghanistan, Pakistan, European Union markets
himachal-pradesh_kangra.gi_info: Kangra tea has unique Geographical Indicator (GI) recognised by the European Union
himachal-pradesh_kangra.key_facts: Current area under cultivation is 1,400 hectares (625 hectares large estates, 775 hectares small growers) | Area under tea cultivation was 5,000 hectares under British rule | Number of farmers reduced from more than 3,000 to half | Annual production in 2013 was 10.49 lakh kg, 2019 was 9.54 lakh kg, 2020 was 10.87 lakh kg | Production decades ago was 17 to 18 lakh kg | Tea is grown at 900-1,400 m above sea level on Dhauladhar mountain slopes | Over 900 hectares of land under tea plantations lay inactive | Average tea yield in Kangra is 230 kg per hectare against 1,800 kg per hectare national average | Kangra tea not fetching price of Rs 200 per kg | Mechanisation introduced from 2014-15 onwards to address labour issues | Approximately 200-300 hectare abandoned area now in pluckable state due to mechanisation

himachal-pradesh_kinnaur.product_varieties: Himachali Chulli Oil, Chulli (wild apricot beverage), Zardalu (regional subtype), Share (Ladakh subtype)
himachal-pradesh_kinnaur.raw_materials: wild apricot fruit (Prunus armeniaca L.), apples, phab (traditional fermentation starter)
himachal-pradesh_kinnaur.gi_info: Himachali Chulli Oil registered under Geographical Indications (GIs) of Goods Act, 1999 as of May 30, 2019
himachal-pradesh_kinnaur.key_facts: Chulli is a traditional alcoholic beverage from Kinnaur district of Himachal Pradesh | Fruit ripens from green to bright orange-red or yellow, typically June to August in Himalayan regions | Wild apricot trees grow at high-altitude regions between 1,500 and 3,500 meters on rocky slopes | GI registration prevents unauthorised production and misuse of the name | Unauthorised use of the GI can result in maximum 3-year imprisonment and fine of maximum Rs 2,00,000 | Himachal Pradesh was first state to formulate a policy for GI registration vide notification dated September 10, 2004 | Chulli exhibits sour-sweet flavor profile with higher malic acid content compared to domesticated varieties | Acidity ranges from 1.6–3.4% and sugar content from 6.30–8.50%

himachal-pradesh_kullu.product_varieties: shawls, caps, woolen garments, jackets, stoles, laces, borders, socks, suits, ponchos, wool pajamas
himachal-pradesh_kullu.key_facts: Handloom weaving is the traditional art and craft of the Himachali people of Kullu region inherited from ancestors | The tradition originated to satisfy local demand but gradually led to commercialization | Weavers have made changes in designs and motifs according to modern buyers' needs | The industry is the backbone of the economic structure of the region | Most local inhabitants of Kullu are engaged in traditional occupation of weaving and spinning woolen garments | Fine quality and harmonious color combination of Kullu shawls testify to enriched art and craft | Kullu caps bear designs from latest fashion | Colorful and attractive woolen wear has internationally acclaimed status | Products come from small and medium scale cottage industries

himachal-pradesh_lahaul--spiti.product_varieties: juice, squashes, teas, jams, seeds oil
himachal-pradesh_lahaul--spiti.success_story_detail: Started procuring and processing in 2014 with 100 kg; plans increased to 500 kg; achieves 6-7 litres of concentrate per 10 kg berries; margins as high as 500 per cent
himachal-pradesh_lahaul--spiti.raw_materials: seabuckthorn berries, seabuckthorn seeds, seabuckthorn leaves
himachal-pradesh_lahaul--spiti.key_facts: 70 women from Rangrik engaged in collection and sale | Berries collected in August and September | Women earn additional income of around Rs 8,000 per season | Berries sold to Spiti Sea-buckthorn Society for Rs 63/kg | In 20 years, collections increased nearly 30 times | Collectors paid four-five times more than historical rates | Dried leaves fetch close to Rs 300/kg | Seabuckthorn juice held 7 per cent beverage market share at its peak in mid-2000s, reduced to 5 per cent today | DRDO developed and popularised seabuckthorn juice technology in early 2000s | ODOP project start date: 2021-03-01

himachal-pradesh_mandi.product_varieties: almirahs, file cabinets, steel racks, showcases, tables, chairs
himachal-pradesh_mandi.success_story_detail: Abdul Matin: 30 years in steel furniture business, shop closed during pandemic | Jewel Ahmed: 20 years in sector, sales dropped from Tk7-8 lakh/month to Tk60,000-70,000/month
himachal-pradesh_mandi.scheme_names: Government stimulus package (Tk72,750 crore announced in April 2020, Tk20,000 crore allocated for MSME)
himachal-pradesh_mandi.key_facts: Steel furniture industry valued at Tk10,000 crore | Sales plummeted by 60-70% during pandemic | 4-5 shops already closed in Nababpur cluster | Steel furniture industry excluded from SME Foundation stimulus package list | Competition from wood, laminated board, and plastic furniture caused gradual decline before pandemic

himachal-pradesh_shimla.product_varieties: walking sticks, jewelry boxes, temple replicas, wooden toys, spinning tops, miniature trains, kitchen implements, rolling pins, spice boxes, wooden chessboards, folding screens, small tables, mirror frames, carved wooden objects, figurines of Hindu deities
himachal-pradesh_shimla.scheme_names: Jawaharlal Nehru National Urban Renewal Mission (JnNURM)
himachal-pradesh_shimla.raw_materials: Himalayan walnut, deodar cedar, brass inlay
himachal-pradesh_shimla.key_facts: Lakkar Bazaar has been selling carved wooden objects for well over a century | The market can be walked in ten minutes if you don't stop | Many shops are family operations that have passed through three or four generations | The market sits along a ridge that extends from the western end of the Mall Road, near the old Gaiety Theatre | By the early twentieth century, it had established itself as Shimla's primary destination for wooden goods | Shimla became the summer capital of British India in 1864 | After independence in 1947, the market shifted its focus toward domestic tourists and pilgrims heading to nearby temples | Colonial-era municipal restrictions have limited high-rise development along the ridge, helping preserve the market

himachal-pradesh_sirmaur.product_varieties: Slotted boxes, self-erecting formats, single wall boxes, double wall boxes, triple wall boxes, printed shipping boxes, ESD cardboard boxes, heavy-duty packaging, pallet boxes
himachal-pradesh_sirmaur.raw_materials: Kraft paper, softwood trees, fluted material, outer liner paper, inner liner paper
himachal-pradesh_sirmaur.key_facts: Global corrugated box production reached 400 million tons in 2022 | The global corrugated packaging market was valued at $410.2 billion in 2023 with a CAGR of 4.2% from 2023 to 2030 | Asia accounts for 55% of total global production | Corrugated packaging is 90% recyclable with 45% of global production using post-consumer recycled fiber as of 2023 | The corrugated boxes market in India hit USD 7.8 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 18.6 billion by 2033 at a 9.6% CAGR | The broader corrugated packaging market in India stood at USD 25 billion in 2024 | E-commerce accounted for 32% of corrugated packaging demand in 2023 | The food and beverage sector consumed 29% of global corrugated packaging in 2023 | Automated corrugated box-making machines now achieve 400 boxes per minute as of 2023 | Corrugated cardboard is made up of fluted material, outer liner, and inner liner components

himachal-pradesh_solan.daily_output: 3 tonnes (button mushroom production at Faldakot unit)
himachal-pradesh_solan.unit_count: 2 (SOLANi Agro has mushroom production unit and waste recycling/compost unit)
himachal-pradesh_solan.product_varieties: Button mushroom, Shiitake, Oyster, King oyster, Lion's mane, Reishi, Cordyceps, Chanterelles, Gucchi (wild mushrooms)
himachal-pradesh_solan.success_story_name: SOLANi Agro, Planet Mushroom (Poonam Sharma and Naveen Patwal)
himachal-pradesh_solan.success_story_detail: SOLANi Agro achieved ₹1.67 crore turnover in 2023-24 starting from necessity-driven activity during COVID-19 pandemic | Planet Mushroom: Built Rs 15 crore business of exotic mushrooms | Naveen Patwal expanded button mushroom farm daily capacity from 800 kg to 3 tonnes by 2016, repaid Rs 1.35 crore bank loan by 2012 | Fresh exotic mushrooms sell at Rs 1,000-1,200 per kg, dried exotic mushrooms fetch Rs 8,000 wholesale and Rs 12,000 retail
himachal-pradesh_solan.raw_materials: Agricultural residues (crop residues), paddy straw, agricultural waste (250-300 tonnes annually recycled)
himachal-pradesh_solan.key_facts: Button mushroom has shelf life of only 3 days | Exotic mushrooms more flavorful with unique textures, aromas, and nutritional properties | SOLANi Agro waste recycling unit processes 250-300 tonnes of crop residue annually using chamber-based decomposition system | India mushroom market valued at USD 1,610.89 million in 2025, projected USD 3,023.86 million by 2035 at 6.8% CAGR | Button mushroom segment held 73% market share in India in 2025 | Fresh segment dominated Indian mushroom market with 64.1% share | Urban households purchase 200g mushroom pack with frequency of 2.5 times per month (as of 2025) | HoReCa sector absorbs 40% of total mushroom volume in metro cities

jammu-and-kashmir_anantnag.product_varieties: fish balls, fish curry, fish kabab, fish pickle, ready to eat fish sauce, canned fish products
jammu-and-kashmir_anantnag.scheme_names: PMMSY, HADP, State Aquaculture Dev. Scheme, Fisheries Infrastructure Dev. Fund, ODOP (PMFME)
jammu-and-kashmir_anantnag.key_facts: District Anantnag generated total production of 553 metric tonnes in government and private sector | Common incubation centre (CIC) under ODOP at Achabal Anantnag established at project cost of 2.45 crores | District designated as Trout District of the country | CIC equipped with descaling, degutting, slicing, maceration, packaging, canning systems | Machinery converts fish waste into fish feed | 50% subsidy provided in some government schemes | District has innumerable fresh water resources with tremendous potential for trout fish production in private sector
jammu-and-kashmir_anantnag._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

jammu-and-kashmir_bandipora.key_facts: Crewal based shawl was previously communicated as ODOP for Bandipora but was not actually practiced in the district | Bandipora has more than three lakh sheep and 7,200 registered breeders | Sheep emerged as a strong contender for ODOP given the district's sizeable livestock base | Nearly half of local mutton consumption is met within the district | SKUAST has ongoing initiatives aimed at strengthening the Gurezi sheep breed | Additional potential products discussed include honey, water chestnuts, lotus stem and Gurezi potato

jammu-and-kashmir_budgam.success_story_name: Abdul Rashid Sofi, Farooq Ahmed Reshi
jammu-and-kashmir_budgam.success_story_detail: Abdul Rashid Sofi is a young artisan from Arwah village in Beerwah tehsil, Budgam, who belongs to a family of Kani weavers | Farooq Ahmed Reshi, a skilled Kani weaver from Budgam, earns a maximum of Rs 500 per day
jammu-and-kashmir_budgam.raw_materials: pashmina wool from Changra goats | wool or pashm
jammu-and-kashmir_budgam.key_facts: Weaving a Kani shawl can take from eight months to several years | Average price of an authentic Kani shawl starts at Rs 1 lakh and goes up to several lakhs | Kani weaving skill dates back to 14th century when Persian mystic Mir Sayyid Ali Hamdani introduced various crafts to Kashmir | In 18th and 19th centuries, Kani shawls gained popularity among Europe's elite | Empress Joséphine was captivated by their elegance, sparking a trend through French aristocracy | Napoleon gifted masterpieces to Joséphine on their marriage | Wool fibre diameter is 12–15 microns | Kani shawls use tiny wooden needles called Kanis (wooden spools) | Each Kani shawl is entirely handwoven, involving no machines or synthetic dyes
jammu-and-kashmir_budgam._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

jammu-and-kashmir_doda.success_story_detail: Started lavender cultivation in 2010 on 2 kanals; expanded to 28 kanals under cultivation and 15 kanals for nurseries by March 2023
jammu-and-kashmir_doda.key_facts: Area under commercial Floriculture 40 hectares total (20 ha Small Lavender Nurseries + 20 ha Lavender Plants area) | Lavender Nursery planting material introduction with approx. 108 Lacs investment | Bharat Bushan owns 10 kanals own land and leased 30 kanals for total 40 kanals land | Modern technology adopted includes quality planting material, intercultural operations, proper harvesting and essential oil extraction

jammu-and-kashmir_jammu.export_countries: Iran, Afghanistan, Gulf nations, Middle East, Iraq, United Arab Emirates, United States
jammu-and-kashmir_jammu.export_value: approximately 60 lakh tonnes during 2024-25 | ₹50,000 crore worth
jammu-and-kashmir_jammu.key_facts: India is the world's leading exporter of basmati rice commanding over 70% of global production | Punjab and Haryana contribute nearly 75% of total premium aromatic basmati grain exported from the country | Haryana's share is roughly 35% and Punjab accounts for 40% of total exports | India exported approximately 60 lakh tonnes of basmati rice during 2024-25 with demand primarily driven by Middle East and West Asian markets

jammu-and-kashmir_kathua.raw_materials: fine undercoat fibers of Changthangi goat
jammu-and-kashmir_kathua.key_facts: Basohli School of Painting is the first School of Pahari Painting and significantly important School in Indian Paintings | Basohli is situated on the Right Bank of River Ravi at an altitude of about 1876 ft | Raja Bhupat Pal rebuilt the ancient settlement of Basohli | Basohli Paintings were first mentioned in Archaeological Survey of India report in 1918-19, published in 1921 | Global pashmina shawls market was valued at $2.8 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach $4.9 billion by 2034 at CAGR of 6.4% | Woman Style segment held largest share at 48.2% in 2025 | Asia Pacific dominated with 38.5% revenue share in 2025 | Pashmina shawls are crafted from fine undercoat fibers of Changthangi goat native to Himalayan plateaus of Kashmir, Ladakh, and parts of Nepal
jammu-and-kashmir_kathua._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article

jammu-and-kashmir_kishtwar.cluster_names: Matta, Hidyal, Tund, Nagini, Bera-Bhatta, Begana, Hatta, Pochhal
jammu-and-kashmir_kishtwar.product_varieties: Kishtwar Gucchi Kesar, Shahi Kishtwar saffron, Kishtwari saffron
jammu-and-kashmir_kishtwar.key_facts: District Kishtwar was created in 2007 and started functioning as independent administrative unit on 01-04-2007 | Kishtwar is located 235 kms from Jammu, the winter capital of Jammu and Kashmir | District altitude varies from 3000-15000 ft. above sea level | Kishtwar is known as 'Land of Sapphire and Saffron' | Area: 7737 Sq. Kms | Out of 156 revenue villages in Kishtwar district, saffron is cultivated in villages including Matta, Hidyal, Tund, Nagini, Bera-Bhatta, Begana, Hatta, Pochhal, and L | Kishtwar saffron is cultivated at altitude of 5351 feet above sea level | Saffron is also known as 'KESAR' in India, 'Kum-Kum' or 'Lohit' in Sanskrit, 'KUNG' in Kishtwari, and 'Zafron' in Persian | Botanical name: Crocus sativa
jammu-and-kashmir_kishtwar._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

jammu-and-kashmir_kulgam.success_story_detail: Garlic Manufacturers and Wholesale Suppliers and Exporters in Kulgam
jammu-and-kashmir_kulgam.key_facts: Garlic (Allium sativum) is one of the important bulb crops grown and used as a spice or condiment throughout India | Garlic is rich in proteins, phosphorous, potassium, calcium, magnesium and carbohydrates | Ascorbic acid content is very high in green garlic | Garlic has been cultivated for thousands of years | Original abode of garlic is Central Asia and Southern Europe especially Mediterranean region | Average price of Garlic in Kulgam Mandi is ₹4,618 per quintal (as of 1 July 2025) | Minimum price observed was ₹2,500 per quintal and maximum price recorded was ₹7,000 per quintal | Retail price estimate is ₹25.00 - ₹70.00 per kg in local market | Garlic contains about 0.1% volatile oil | Diallyl disulfide comprises 60% of volatile oil constituents
jammu-and-kashmir_kulgam._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

jammu-and-kashmir_kupwara.production_scale: 27.22 lakh tonnes (2022) for overall horticulture sector; walnuts contribute nearly 12% to total fruit production
jammu-and-kashmir_kupwara.employment_total: 35 lakh people directly or indirectly in horticulture sector
jammu-and-kashmir_kupwara.cluster_names: Shopian, Baramulla, Kupwara (apple hubs); Budgam, Srinagar (walnut wood crafts)
jammu-and-kashmir_kupwara.product_varieties: walnuts, almonds, apples, cherries, pears, plums, saffron, kala zeera, honey
jammu-and-kashmir_kupwara.export_value: J K designated as export zone for apples and walnuts
jammu-and-kashmir_kupwara.key_facts: Horticulture sector area under cultivation increased from 1.31 lakh hectares (1980) to 3.44 lakh hectares (2022) | Production increased more than fivefold from 5.6 lakh tonnes to 27.22 lakh tonnes | Annual turnover estimated at Rs10,000 crore | Sector contributes 6-7% to GDP | Walnuts occupy 93% of total dry fruit cultivation area | Apple cultivation accounts for 50% of total area under fruit crops and 77% of overall fruit production | Average apple yield is 12.5 tonnes per hectare | J K is leading producer of apples, cherries, walnuts, and almonds in India | District Kupwara is a major apple-producing hub
jammu-and-kashmir_kupwara._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

jammu-and-kashmir_poonch.production_scale: 75.0 metric tonnes from an area of around 507.0 ha
jammu-and-kashmir_poonch.cluster_names: Poonch, Rajouri, Kishtwar, Doda, Ramban, Udhampur, Kathua
jammu-and-kashmir_poonch.gi_info: GI tag mentioned as being pursued to boost emerging pecan nut cultivation industry
jammu-and-kashmir_poonch.key_facts: Pecan nut cultivation in J&K is about a century old | ODOP has identified 1102 products from 761 districts across the country | Scientific name: Caryaillinoensis (Wang) C. Koch, Family: Juglandaceae | Pecan nut originated in North America, cultivation started in India in 1937 | Poonch and Rajouri are main districts suitable for cultivation | Pecans mainly grown in Jammu and Kashmir and Himachal Pradesh | Many improved cultivar orchards recently planted will give commercial yield in 3-4 years | Pecan nut called Khori, Shimla akhrot, and Bachh Doon in J&K | Can be grown in areas not suitable for apple and walnut cultivation

jammu-and-kashmir_pulwama.gi_info: Geographical Indication Tag (GI) to Kashmir Saffron Under GI No 635 and Certificate No 366 dated 1-05-2020
jammu-and-kashmir_pulwama.key_facts: Saffron has been selected as ODOP product from district Pulwama | Saffron is a perennial herb known for its aroma and taste | In India, most of the saffron production is limited to Jammu and Kashmir | Saffron is popularly known as red gold | Saffron is used in food processing, pharmaceuticals, cosmetics, perfumery and textile dyes | Saffron is one of the world's most expensive spices | Global saffron market size was valued at USD 374.6 million in 2020 | Global saffron market is expected to expand at a CAGR of 8.5% from 2020 to 2028 | Main active compounds in saffron include picrocrocin, safranal, and crocin | HSN Code for saffron export is 091020
jammu-and-kashmir_pulwama._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

jammu-and-kashmir_rajouri.key_facts: Walnut market size was valued at USD 9.0 billion in 2025 and is estimated to reach USD 9.68 billion in 2026 | Asia-Pacific region dominated the walnut market with 65% value share in 2025 | Shelled premium-grade kernels are growing at 9.8% annual pace | In-shell walnuts led with 60% of walnut market share in 2025 | Asia-Pacific is projected to record fastest CAGR of 8.0% from 2026 to 2031 | Asia commands significant portion of production value fueled by acreage expansion in China and India | Rajouri district area is 2630 sq km with population of 642,415

jammu-and-kashmir_ramban.product_varieties: Sulai Honey (Plectranthus rugosus), Acacia honey, Mustard honey
jammu-and-kashmir_ramban.success_story_name: Sanglaab Valley Fed Honey Producer Company Limited
jammu-and-kashmir_ramban.success_story_detail: Producer of Ramban Sulai Honey sold on ONDC marketplace at ₹900 for 500 gram pack
jammu-and-kashmir_ramban.raw_materials: Nectar of snow-white blossoms of Sulai (Plectranthus rugosus), Acacia flowers, Mustard flowers, wild flora
jammu-and-kashmir_ramban.key_facts: Sulai honey is white and amber in colour with high keeping quality | Honey is collected during August to October | Plant used in traditional medicine for toothache and claimed effective as antiseptic, hypoglycaemic, anti-diarrheal and bronchodilator | Honey has strong antioxidant and immuno-modulatory potentials | No additives used, natural honey | Nutritional content per serving: 64 kcal, 17g carbohydrates, 0g total lipid fat | Ramban district bounded by Anantnag (South), Udhampur (North), Doda (East), Reasi (West) | Ramban district has abundant Plectranthus rugosus flora locally called Solai
jammu-and-kashmir_ramban._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article

jharkhand_dhanbad.product_varieties: coking coal, metallurgical-grade coal, prime coking coal
jharkhand_dhanbad.key_facts: Dhanbad is known as the Coal Capital of India | Located in Jharkhand state | Home to Jharia Coalfield, one of India's most significant coalfields | Only source of prime coking coal in India | Coal mining dates back over 100 years to British colonial period | Bharat Coking Coal Limited (BCCL) headquartered in Dhanbad | Provides coal to thermal power plants, steel industries, and cement manufacturing | Coal provides approximately 55% of India's thermal power plant energy | Jharia coal fires have been burning for approximately 100 years | IIT (ISM) Dhanbad specializes in mining, petroleum, and earth sciences | Coal mining started in 19th century during British rule | Coal exports occurred to foreign countries due to high calorific value

jharkhand_hazaribagh.cluster_names: Hazaribagh, Isko village of Barkagaon block
jharkhand_hazaribagh.product_varieties: Sohrai Painting wall art, Sohrai Painting hand-painted products (Jute File Folders, Trays, Pen Stands, Tea Coasters, Cutlery Holders)
jharkhand_hazaribagh.raw_materials: natural earth colours (red, black, yellow, white) foraged from wild or purchased from local merchants, chewing twigs used as paint brushes, cloth rags for base coat application, various soils, cow dung, husk and water for mud wall preparation
jharkhand_hazaribagh.key_facts: Sohrai painting is a folk/tribal painting tradition practised mostly in villages of Hazaribagh area in Jharkhand | Traditionally women paint on mud walls of homes during Sohrai festival which falls a day after Diwali | Paintings depict matriarchal tradition passed down from mothers to daughters | Isko rock art site discovered in 1991 by Shri Bulu Imam, dating from 3000-7000 BC from Paleolithic age | Stone tools discovered near Isko rock art site | Sohrai art believed to originate from Meso-chalcolithic period (9000-5000 BCE) or late Stone Age (20000-25000) | Festival celebrated twice a year - once during Diwali on Amavasya in Hindu month of Karthik (October-November) for three days, and once during Poush Sankranti in January | Motifs include animals (peacock, elephant, tiger, crocodile, snake, jackal), flowers, plants, geometric figures and Shiva as Pashupati | Practitioners belong to Kurmi, Santal, Munda, Oraon, Agaria, Ghatwal ethnic groups | Paintings naturally fade away after festival

jharkhand_jamtara.key_facts: Jamtara district area is 1811 sq.km | Jamtara district population is 7,91,042 | Jamtara district is bounded by Deoghar district in north, Dumka and West Bengal in the east, Dhanbad and West Bengal in the south and Giridih in the west | Jamtara is a new district created by separating from Dumka district

maharashtra_yavatmal.key_facts: Yavatmal known as 'Cotton City' in Vidarbha | Cotton ginning mills separate cotton fibers from seed bolls using saw gins and cleaners | Establishing a 24-gin mill requires investment of 9.64 crore rupees | 24-gin mill projected to reach full capacity in 5 years with 27.05% internal rate of return | Cotton sector faces challenges from bollworm pest infestations and global cotton price fluctuations | 25 approved Common Facility Centres (CFCs) and 16 existing ones across Maharashtra ODOP scheme

manipur_imphal-east.production_scale: Rs. 2350 lakhs (approximately $3.2 million USD) annual turnover
manipur_imphal-east.unit_count: 1850 looms, of which 40% are working currently
manipur_imphal-east.cluster_names: Wangkhei Konsam Leikai Handlooms Cluster, Irilbung Sub-division, Imphal East District
manipur_imphal-east.product_varieties: cotton and acrylic shawls, bed sheets, lungis, mufflers, dresses, school bags, Inaphee (chaddar), Phanek (sarong/ladies' lungies), dhoti, saree, skirts
manipur_imphal-east.success_story_detail: Designer from Manipur who uses GI-tagged Wangkhei Phee among handloom fabrics; the fabric has been worn by Bollywood actors Sushmita Sen and Kangana Ranaut
manipur_imphal-east.scheme_names: Integrated Handloom Development Scheme, One District One Product (ODOP)
manipur_imphal-east.raw_materials: Lashing (Cotton ball), Kabrang (Mulberry cocoon), bark of Santhak tree species, plant extracts, flowers, bark and leaves for dyes, rice-based starch for sizing
manipur_imphal-east.key_facts: Wangkhei Phee was first developed by skilled weavers in Wangkhei area for the royal family | The fabric is made with very fine white cotton yarn with closely woven texture | Majority of weavers living below the poverty line | Designs include khoi (hook), thambal (lotus), atargulap (rose), Angom Ningthou (royal symbol), khongup melei (orchid), numit chura (sunrise head gear) | Weaving process uses fly shuttle loom and throw shuttle loom methods
manipur_imphal-east._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article

manipur_imphal-west.product_varieties: handmade cloth dolls, traditional Laiphadibi dolls, contemporary Laiphadibi dolls
manipur_imphal-west.success_story_name: Konsam Ibomcha, Soibam Birla Devi, Mayanglambam Sumati, Khongbantabam Thoibi
manipur_imphal-west.success_story_detail: Konsam Ibomcha conferred Padma Shri Award in 2022 for distinguished services in Arts and recognition of dying local art of Laiphadibi making | Khongbantabam Thoibi is a state handicraft-award winner, age 66, with six children
manipur_imphal-west.raw_materials: cloth pieces, old cloth, discarded cloth from local tailor, mud, wood, straw, black thread, red thread, beads, laces
manipur_imphal-west.key_facts: Laiphadibi means doll where Lai=God, Phadi=dilapidated piece of cloth, Bi=feminine gender | Dolls are believed to have living spirits and are treated as beings with feelings | Children call them Ita meaning female companion | Dolls are put to sleep in Lubak bamboo baskets after play | Original laiphadibi lacked detailed facial features with only a colourful vertical thread across the face | Today dolls have eyes, brows, hair made with black thread, nose and lips stitched in red | A ritual called boriba must be followed where permission and blessings are sought by offering nominal fee to the teacher | Elders believe if boriba ritual is not observed, the doll cannot see and speak
manipur_imphal-west._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

manipur_tamenglong.key_facts: Tamenglong district produces more than 80 percent of total orange production in Manipur | Over 40 percent of land area in district is under orange cultivation | Tamenglong district located at elevation of 1,290 m above mean sea level | Average orange weight is about 233 g | Fruit has 10 to 14 segments | High juice content of about 45.75 percent | TSS of 11.93 °Bx | Harvest period between November to February | Annual Tamenglong Orange Festival hosted since 2003 | 18th State Level Orange Festival held in 2025 | Cultivated solely using organic means without chemical fertilizers or pesticides

manipur_tengnoupal.key_facts: Manipur has 53 bamboo species out of 136 reported in India | Bamboo occupies 10,687 sq km of total growing area (61.36% of state forest area) | Growing stock of 15,469 tonnes from state (second largest in Northeast India's 8 states) | Bamboo locally called 'Wa' in Manipur | Bamboo shoots locally called 'Usoi' | Only 110 out of 1250 worldwide bamboo species have edible shoots | India is world's second largest bamboo producer after China | Bamboo shoots are light yellow colored with strong smell and bitter taste | Bamboo shoots are rich in protein and fiber | New shoots produced in June/July | Bamboo needed from birth to death in local culture

meghalaya_eastern-west-khasi-hills.production_scale: India's kiwi fruit market valued at nearly US$ 150 million in 2024
meghalaya_eastern-west-khasi-hills.product_varieties: Fresh kiwi, kiwi juices, dried slices, kiwi powders, kiwi concentrates, value-added kiwi beverages
meghalaya_eastern-west-khasi-hills.key_facts: Domestic production meets less than one-third of annual demand | Acreage expanded from 3,500 ha in 2020 to 5,000 ha in 2024 | Himachal Pradesh leads with 60% production share, followed by Uttarakhand (20%), Jammu & Kashmir (5%) | India relies on imports for 70% of consumption | Post-harvest losses improved from 20% to 12% | Cold-chain infrastructure stretches over 3,000 km by 2024 | Processed kiwi segment growing at nearly 15% CAGR | Online fruit sales surged to nearly 20% of demand | Per-hectare yields improving from 12 tons to 17 tons | Government injected INR 100 crore under National Horticulture Mission for planting material, irrigation, and farmer training | Imported fruit costs US$ 1.8–2.0 per kg compared to domestic output at US$ 1.5 | Consumption expected to cross 120,000 thousand tonnes by 2032 | Metro cities (Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru) account for half of demand | Tier-2 cities seeing 18% annual growth

meghalaya_north-garo-hills.key_facts: Organic farming yields are approximately 18% lower than chemical-based cultivation | Organic systems reduce input costs by 33% | Organic practices result in 14.3% higher net income per hectare | Study was conducted during 2023-2024 in selected banana-growing villages of North Garo Hills District | Study assessed 50 farmers | Organic practices improved soil fertility, enhanced biodiversity and strengthened overall farmer livelihoods
meghalaya_north-garo-hills._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

meghalaya_west-garo-hills.key_facts: Cashewnut production plays a pivot role in fostering and sustaining rural development in West Garo Hills | Adoption of new technology and sustainable utilization of resources can help cashewnut growers in minimizing cost of production | Meghalaya has become very popular in organic farming which gives good returns from the Garo Hills region as well as export market | India has an area of 1027 thousand ha under cashew with an estimated annual production of about 725.42 thousand MT of raw cashew nut | Commercial cultivation of cashew is taken up in eight states mainly in west and eastern coast viz., Andhra Pradesh, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, Maharashtra, Orissa, Tamil Nadu and West Bengal | Cashew is also grown in few pockets of Assam, Chattisgarh, Gujarat, Meghalaya, Nagaland and Tripura

He is setting up Ceramic Studio in Mairang with government funding for Ateliers.

nagaland_peren.production_scale: ₹60 lakhs estimated annual produce (14,000 kgs) for current year; ₹1 crore turnover in 2022

nagaland_peren.employment_total: 200 households were Naga Mircha farmers at Seiyhama (as of 2022)
nagaland_peren.cluster_names: Seiyhama village, Botsa block, Zeliangrong region
nagaland_peren.product_varieties: Naga Mircha (also known as Raja Mircha, Bhoot Jolokia, Ghost Pepper, U Morok)
nagaland_peren.success_story_detail: 200 households cultivating Naga mircha; collected ₹27 lakh revenue in 2022; expecting ₹1 crore in 2023; each household earning minimum ₹60,000-₹70,000 annually; larger scale farmers earning ₹4-5 lakhs annually; 2000 kgs sold at 3rd festival in 2024
nagaland_peren.raw_materials: soil with ideal water content, sunlight, bamboo and banana plantations, no chemical pesticides or fertilizers used
nagaland_peren.gi_info: Naga Mircha received Geographical Indication (GI) recognition in 2008; first product to receive GI tag from Nagaland
nagaland_peren.key_facts: Naga Mircha is widely grown in Peren, Mon, Kohima, and Dimapur | Originated in Zeliangrong region of Nagaland | Ranked in top five SHU-based chillies worldwide | Botsa block selected as focus location spanning 100 hectares | 3rd organic King Chilli Festival held September 6, 2024 | Educated youth and graduate/post-graduate students engaged in cultivation | About 45 km from Nagaland's capital Kohima

nagaland_tuensang.success_story_detail: One tin of seeds produces more than 15 tins of harvest in Sotokur, compared to only 8 tins in Sangsangyu
nagaland_tuensang.key_facts: Kholar is a high-value legume crop widely cultivated in Nagaland | FBMP-KfW project spans 64 villages in five districts, covering 13 Community Conserved Areas and benefitting about 35,000 households | Improved kholar cultivation techniques include proper spacing, planting in stony soil with minimal manure, optimal seed depth, seed variety selection based on altitude, intercropping with maize, and standalone planting | FBMP launched in 2020 and is funded by German Development Bank (KfW) | Training session on kholar cultivation took place at Sangsangyu village on August 23

nagaland_wokha.key_facts: Wokha is the largest producer of bananas in Nagaland | Annual production approximately 11,000-11,500 metric tons | Banana cultivation spans approximately 864 hectares | 14 farmers and 5 officials trained at National Research Centre for Banana (NRCB), Tamil Nadu | Banana Festival scheduled for October 22-24 | Branding under 'Wokha Banana' label with barcoding and certification | Online platforms include Amazon Karigar, Flipkart Samarth, and ONDC | All parts of banana plant utilized for economic value | District's subtropical climate suitable for banana cultivation | Memorandum of Understanding with ICAR-NRCB for technology transfer | Agri-Business Incubation Centre proposed at ICAR-KVK Wokha with NABARD support

odisha_bargarh.production_scale: ₹64.00 crore per annum (Sambalpuri Bastralaya HLCS Ltd turnover)
odisha_bargarh.daily_output: 200 bundles per day (Dyeing unit capacity at Bargarh)
odisha_bargarh.artisan_count: 38,298 nos. of weavers | 39,000 weavers employed across 48 primary weavers co-operative societies
odisha_bargarh.unit_count: 48 nos. of primary weavers co-operative society | 18,164 household units
odisha_bargarh.product_varieties: sarees, Bedsheet, Dress materials, Napkins, Lungi, furnishing items, tie dye design Sambalpuri Saree, dhotis, Lungies, Bed-covers, furnishing materials | more than 3000 varieties
odisha_bargarh.success_story_name: Sambalpuri Bastralaya Handloom Co-operative Society Ltd, Bargarh
odisha_bargarh.success_story_detail: largest primary weavers co-operative society of the State functioning since 1954 | Production-cum-marketing unit with annual turnover about Rs.64.00 crore | Dyeing unit capacity of 200 bundles per day
odisha_bargarh.scheme_names: Promotion of Handloom Industries Scheme (POHI), Mukhya Mantri Bayana Jyoti Yayona (MBJY), Barista Bunakar Sahayata Yojana (BBSY), Bunakar Assistance for Livelihood and Income Augmentation (BALIA), Cluster Development Programme
odisha_bargarh.raw_materials: cotton yarn ranging from 2/80's to 2/120's mercerised yarn, silk yarn, tassar yarn
odisha_bargarh.key_facts: Handloom is a prominent cottage industry providing employment next to agriculture sector | Weavers producing with cotton yarn and mercerised yarn mostly | Society participates in Various Expos and Exhibitions inside and outside the state | Sambalpuri handloom has historical importance and cultural relevance | Study indicates robust handloom industry supporting local livelihoods
odisha_bargarh._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article

odisha_bhadrak.scheme_names: National Food Security Mission, Bringing Green Revolution in Eastern India, National Mission on Agricultural Extension and Technology, National Mission on Sustainable Agriculture (NMSA), Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana
odisha_bhadrak.key_facts: Bhadrak produced 2,505 kg/ha of rice in 2015-16, among the highest in the country | Despite drought in 2015-16, Jagatsinghpur and Bhadrak produced 2,571 kg/ha and 2,505 kg/ha respectively | Odisha rice yield was 1,491 kg/ha in 2015-16, down from 1,989 kg/ha in 2014-15 | Bargarh is known as the rice bowl of Odisha with highest rice production for past 10 years | Other high-producing districts include Boudh, Dhenkanal, Nabarangapur, Rayagada, Sambalpur, Sonepur and Sundargarh
odisha_bhadrak._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

odisha_boudh.product_varieties: Berhampuri Patta, Phoda Kumbha sarees, jodas (matching saree and stole for men)
odisha_boudh.gi_info: GI tagged due to unique weave linked with culture, traditions, and history of geographical location
odisha_boudh.key_facts: Berhampuri Patta also known as Phoda Kumbha sarees have history dating back to 14th century AD | Introduced by Mohuri king in Berhampur town in Odisha | Berhampur is known as the silk city of India | Made in pairs - a sari for women and matching joda for men | Characterized by temple style designs known as 'phoda kumbha' or 'badhi kumbha' | Two weavers work on each throw shuttle pit loom to weave the phoda kumbha at the border | Saris were once worn by brides for wedding ceremony | Carries 200 years tradition in its weaving pattern | Unique for thick silk with special texture | Now in declining state
odisha_boudh._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

odisha_cuttack.production_scale: ₹40 crore worth of silver filigree works used for Durga Puja decorations; ₹20 crore gold ornaments used for decorating idols
odisha_cuttack.artisan_count: approximately 2000 artisans from 500 families; 50-60 artisan families depend on silver filigree work as primary livelihood
odisha_cuttack.unit_count: 143 show-rooms at Baniasahi, Nayasadak, Mahammadia Bazar
odisha_cuttack.cluster_names: Mangalabag, Naya Bazaar, Baniasahi, Nayasadak, Mahammadia Bazar
odisha_cuttack.product_varieties: Hair pin, Sinthi, Nose Pin, Ear Ring, Jhumko, Bangles, Chudi, Chud, Chain, Locket, Ornaments, Decorative items, Utility items, Chandi Medha (silver filigree backdrops)
odisha_cuttack.success_story_name: Pankaj Kumar Sahoo, Sudarshan Behera (Sudam Babu), Pradeep Kumar Prusty
odisha_cuttack.success_story_detail: Pankaj Kumar Sahoo is a second-generation artisan whose family has practised this craft for over four decades; silver preparation can reach 200-300 kilos depending on order; Sudarshan Behera has more than four decades of experience; Pradeep Kumar Prusty initiated a Chandi Medha project in April 2024 with support from five co-artisans, now in final phase
odisha_cuttack.scheme_names: Chandi Medha project, Common Facility Center (CFC) - ₹10-crore project
odisha_cuttack.raw_materials: pure silver drawn into wire in different gauges (26 gauges to 36 gauges)
odisha_cuttack.key_facts: The craft has echoed for over five centuries | Silver filigree craft is locally known as tarakasi | A silver piece takes about one year or more depending on size and complexity | One silver piece requires four artisans working together | Wages range between ₹10,000 to ₹15,000 rupees depending on work | Deputy Chief Minister Pravati Parida laid foundation stone for Common Facility Center on CDA premises | PM Narendra Modi praised Cuttack's silver filigree during recent foreign visit | Cuttack is one of two main production centres for silver filigree in India (other is Karimnagar in Andhra Pradesh) | Orders placed for cleaning and refurbishing older tableaux typically takes 10-12 days | In 2025, city features 37 tableaux
odisha_cuttack._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

odisha_kendrapara.key_facts: The craft is indigenous to Kendrapara where golden grass is found in abundance | The golden grass craft is century-old | Around 300 self help groups comprising around 3,000 women and girls have been making and selling golden grass products | Government will build proper roads, training centres, worksheds and houses for the craftspersons under the project | Artisans have limited or no access to skill development programmes and market linkage

odisha_koraput.success_story_detail: Producing eco-friendly Holi colours from natural ingredients; prepared nearly one quintal of natural colours this season

odisha_koraput.key_facts: Tulsi SHG members located in Dumuriput village under Koraput block | Two members received specialised training in Bhubaneswar under Odisha Livelihoods Mission nearly two years ago | Women's groups from Kotpad and Pottangi blocks also involved in production and marketing | ORMAS extended marketing support enabling access to urban markets across Koraput district | Temporary outlet opened in front of zilla parishad office in Koraput town
