odisha_malkangiri.success_story_detail: Top Food Grain Manufacturing Company in Jharkhand, India; manufacturer and wholesale supplier of millets in Malkangiri
odisha_malkangiri.key_facts: Millets contain minerals like magnesium, iron, phosphorus, and potassium | Millets reduce blood pressure and decrease cholesterol | Millets are nutrient-dense, non-glutinous, acid-forming grains | Millets are easier to digest and release lower glucose percentage over long period | Millets are favoured due to great production and short growing season in hot and high temperature | Malkangiri district area is 5791 square kilometers with 1045 villages
odisha_malkangiri._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

odisha_mayurbhanj.key_facts: Rose matta Rice is locally known as Mugei Chaula | It is a traditional rice variety cultivated in Mayurbhanj, Odisha | The rice has a distinct reddish hue signifying high antioxidant content, particularly anthocyanins | It is naturally gluten-free | It contains notable iron content | The rice variety is well-adapted to local agro-climatic conditions of Mayurbhanj | Traditional rice varieties including Rose matta Rice exhibit better water-use efficiency compared to some modern varieties | The rice is an integral part of culinary traditions and cultural celebrations in Mayurbhanj
odisha_mayurbhanj._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

odisha_nabarangpur.production_scale: 7.3 lakh MT estimated production in 2021-22 for Odisha state; Nabarangpur contributed 242.89 thousand MT in 2020-21
odisha_nabarangpur.product_varieties: High Yielding Variety (HYV) maize - 92% usage in Odisha
odisha_nabarangpur.export_countries: Chhattisgarh, West Bengal, Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka
odisha_nabarangpur.export_value: Around two-thirds of produced maize is traded; 92% in Kharif season
odisha_nabarangpur.key_facts: Nabarangpur contributes over 30% of Odisha's total maize production | Maize is the 2nd important cereal in Odisha | Nine districts contribute around 82% of total state production | Cropped in more than 2 lakh Ha of land in Odisha | Nabarangpur maize area: 60,496 ha in 2020-21, increasing to 62,366 ha in 2022-23 | Nabarangpur average yield: 54-55 quintals per hectare | Total maize production in Nabarangpur: 339,285 MT (2020-21), 326,104 MT (2021-22), 333,658 MT (2022-23) | NFSM assistance in Nabarangpur: FY 20-21: 2640 HA / Rs 158.40 lakh; FY 21-22: 2167 HA / Rs 130.02 lakh; FY 22-23: 1002 HA / Rs 60.12 lakh | Maize moisture requirement: 14% max | Fungus content: 1% max | Broken/Damaged/Discolored/Immature: 7.5% max | Weevilled: 0.5% max | Foreign Matter: 2% max | Industry usage: 60% animal & poultry feed, 20% food, 14% starch, 6% export | Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) established to enhance market access
odisha_nabarangpur._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article

odisha_nayagarh.cluster_names: Kantilo (Nayagarh), Bhuban (Dhenkal), Balakati (near Bhubaneswar), Bhatimunda (Cuttack), Belaguntha (Ganjam), Remuna (Balasore), Tarabha (Balangir)
odisha_nayagarh.product_varieties: cooking utensils, plates, bowls, glass tumblers, gong bells, small chimes, lamp stands, Flexible Brass Fish
odisha_nayagarh.raw_materials: copper and tin (ratio approximately 4:1), copper and zinc
odisha_nayagarh.key_facts: Brass and Bell Metal craft is prepared by skilled craftsmen of 'Kansari' community | Dhokra craft is practiced by craftsmen of 'Sithulias' community | Kansari craftsmen were originally residing at Kanyakubja or Kanauj on the bank of Ganga | Gajapati Kapilendra Dev invited Kansari people to Puri to produce brass and bell metal utensils, brass bands and trumpets for Lord Jagannath worship | Craftsmen follow conventional heating and beating methods and lost wax process | Workspace called 'sala' or shed includes stone floor for beating, heating furnace or bhati, raised verandah with cleaning machine | Tools used include mallets, iron blocks, pliers, hand drills, scrubbers | Tradition includes giving bell-metal utility utensils to daughters during marriage in every Odisha household | Preparation procedures are engraved in old palm leaf manuscripts displayed in state museum at Bhubaneswar
odisha_nayagarh._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

odisha_puri.product_varieties: Radha Krishna with Gopikas, Tree of Life Nabagunjara Painting, Krishna Painting, Buddha Painting, Decor Painting, Odissi Dance Form, Mohiniyattam Dance Form, Kathak Dance Form, Manipuri Dance Form, Folk Dance Form, Ganesha
odisha_puri.key_facts: Orissa Pattachitra is based on Hindu mythology and specially inspired by Jagannath and Vaishnava sect | It is an ancient art form | Most Orissa Pattachitra paintings sold at fairs and exhibitions are screen printed or digital prints costing INR 100/print | GiTAGGED paintings are hand-drawn by renowned artists | Product prices range from ₹4,556 to ₹12,155
odisha_puri._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

rajasthan_kota.key_facts: Kota Doria is a fine, lightweight open weave fabric with distinctive square pattern (locally called Khat) | Made from cotton, silk, and zari yarns on pit looms | 300 to 350 Khats made across fabric width with each Khat made by 8 cotton and 6 silk yarns | Traditionally 60% cotton and 40% silk ratio | Originated in Mysore, brought to Kota by Maharao Kishore Singh (also called Rao Kishore Singh, died 1696) | Primarily woven in Kaithoon village, 20 kms from Kota city | Kaithoon has population mostly consisting of weavers | Approximately 1500 weavers work in the cluster | Cotton yarn provides stiffness, silk provides lustre | Ideal for summer wear | Can be decorated with gotta patti, mukesh, mirror work, batik, tie-and-dye, hand-block printing, applique work, and buti (small floral patterns) | Novika Kota Doria Handloom is a manufacturer and wholesaler with 11-25 employees and annual turnover Rs. 50 Lakh - 1 Crore | 2 khats found in an inch (both width and length) in cotton-cotton variety | Each khat has 14 threads (8 cotton, 6 silk) in both cross and longitudinal direction

uttar-pradesh_amethi.key_facts: District Amethi is famous for Moonj production | Amethi lies at latitude 26°9' North and longitude 81°49' East at elevation of 101 meters | District covers 2,329.11 sq.km | District has 4 tehsils (Amethi, Gauriganj, Musafirkhana and Tiloi) | District has 13 blocks and 995 villages | Population (2011 Census): 18,67,678 (Male: 9,45,235, Female: 9,22,443) | Gauriganj is district headquarters | Gomti River drains the district | Climate averages 23°C to 28°C with summers reaching 36°C-44°C and winters dropping to 2°C-3°C | Before 1960, 75% of Amethi was barren but terraforming transformed 80% into cultivable land | District formed on July 1, 2010 as 72nd district of Uttar Pradesh

uttar-pradesh_amroha.key_facts: The Amroha Dholak is a traditional percussion instrument with a double-headed drum design | The right side (dayan) delivers high-pitched tones using goat skin, while the left side (bayan) offers deep bass notes using buffalo leather | Amroha is located in Uttar Pradesh, India | The dholak is integral to North Indian folk music and is used in weddings, festivals, and religious ceremonies | The craft has been passed down through generations of artisans in Amroha | The instrument produces a unique combination of deep, resonant bass and sharp, crisp treble sounds

uttar-pradesh_banda.product_varieties: rings, jewelry, artwork (Taj Mahal), decorative items, wall hangings

uttar-pradesh_banda.raw_materials: Shazar stones from Ken River, wooden bow (Kaman), steel wire, silicon carbide powder, wooden stand (Khunta), gold, silver
uttar-pradesh_banda.key_facts: Shazar is crafted only at Banda in India | Stone slices are 2 to 4 mm thickness | Shazar stones found in only two rivers in world: Ken and Narmada in India | Discovery of rare Shazar stones occurred 400 years ago by Arabs | Queen Victoria took Shazar stones to Britain during East India Company rule | No two Shazar stones are identical | Ken River is the only river in Uttar Pradesh and second river in India that creates colorful patterns in stones | Shazar means 'tree' in Persian | In Arab countries stone is called Haqiq, in India called Sphatik | Craftspeople set stones in gold and silver to increase value | Stones also used in Lucknow jewelry business

uttar-pradesh_siddharthnagar.product_varieties: Kalanamak Rice (Buddha Rice), improved Kalanamak varieties
uttar-pradesh_siddharthnagar.scheme_names: One District One Product (ODOP), Nutrifarm scheme
uttar-pradesh_siddharthnagar.gi_info: G.I. TAG (Geographical Indication) awarded for Kalanamak Rice. Area between 27° 28′ north latitude and 82° 45′ to 83° 10′ east longitude in Uttar Pradesh is considered suitable. Includes District Siddharth Nagar and various blocks in Sant Kabir Nagar, Basti, Gorakhpur, Maharajganj, and Balrampur districts.
uttar-pradesh_siddharthnagar.key_facts: Kalanamak rice has been produced in Siddharthnagar since ancient times, with cultivation since Buddha's time (600 BC). | Until the 1990s, the variety made up more than 10% of total rice cultivation area in Siddharthnagar. | Acreage declined to 0.5% of total rice cultivation by 2002. | Kalanamak Rice contains zinc and iron elements with ability to fight against many diseases. | Kalanamak Rice is softer than Basmati. | In 2013, Kalanamak Rice was selected under the Nutrifarm scheme by the Government of India to fight malnutrition. | Hybrid variety produces 30 quintals of rice while traditional Kalanamak produces 19 quintals. | Normal rice priced at Rs.30 per kg yields Rs.90,000 per hectare, while Kalanamak at Rs.110 per kg yields Rs.209,000 per hectare. | Profit difference is Rs.119,000 per hectare from Kalanamak compared to normal/hybrid variety. | Up to 70 percent rice is obtained ideally in Kalanamak variety. | Kalanamak Rice was previously cultivated in area of about 22,000 hectares in district Siddharth Nagar. | Demonstration activities began from 2017 onwards.
uttar-pradesh_siddharthnagar._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article

uttar-pradesh_sitapur.key_facts: India's handloom industry has 2.8 million looms | Employment of 3.52 million people in direct and allied activities in handloom sector | Handloom Census 2019-20 recorded 35.22 lakh handloom workers employed, with 25.46 lakh women (72.29% share) | 26.73 lakh weavers employed directly and indirectly with 72% being women | India is the world's third-largest consumer of carpets and textile floor coverings with 337 million square meters volume in 2024 | Mats and mattings exported had 12.40% share of total handloom exports during FY23

uttar-pradesh_sultanpur.product_varieties: Rope folding chairs, chairs, tables, pen stands, soups, benas (hand fan), feather footmats, matches, carry-bags, coins, accessories, bracelets, decorates, baskets, artificial jewellery pieces, decorative items, containers for dry storage, food storage devices, carry bags, wall decorators
uttar-pradesh_sultanpur.success_story_name: Kiran Sharma, Buchi Devi, Gyanti Devi, Saidun, Deji Devi, Indravati, Phoolvati Devi, Omtara, Sarita Devi, Sunita, Malti, Kusumkali, Rinku, Guddi Devi, Fatima Bibi
uttar-pradesh_sultanpur.success_story_detail: Fatima Bibi has been practicing this art for over a decade; A group of 50 women from Deshi Deoria, Bhatni and Sonughat villages have come together to create viable commerce from the craft
uttar-pradesh_sultanpur.scheme_names: One District One Product (ODOP), Chief Minister Youth Employment Scheme, One District One Product Margin Money Scheme, One District One Product Training and Toolkit Scheme, Vishwakarma Shram Samman Yojana
uttar-pradesh_sultanpur.raw_materials: Moonj grass (wild grass from Terai area of Gomti River and Yamuna banks), Kaasa grass, natural and powdered colours (red, green, purple, yellow, pink, blue, orange), plastic strips, tinsel, cloth
uttar-pradesh_sultanpur.gi_info: GI tag application filed for Moonj products of Prayagraj; Allahabadi guava is the only product from Prayagraj with a GI tag granted in 2007-2008
uttar-pradesh_sultanpur.key_facts: Moonj products are harvested in October and November | Craft has been practiced for 60 to 70 years | Grass is cut during winter and peel of stalks is left in dew for days to lighten colour | Traditional coiling technique is employed | Sun drying process is used | A group of 50 women created 'Moonj of Purvanchal' initiative supported by Jagriti Enterprise Center | Mainly practiced by women from Nishad community | Production process is based on traditional technology (manual construction) | 18 schemes available | 5 Tehsil, 1 Bandh (Moonj)
uttar-pradesh_sultanpur._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

uttar-pradesh_unnao.production_scale: ₹10 crore annual turnover (as of 2023, down from ₹100 crore in 2010)
uttar-pradesh_unnao.artisan_count: One-third of three lakh artisans (approximately one lakh artisans as of 2022, down from three lakh in 2010)
uttar-pradesh_unnao.unit_count: Less than 100 manufacturing units (down from 200 units in 2010)
uttar-pradesh_unnao.product_varieties: Embroidery on velvets, brocades, silks; sherwani collars; georgette fabric embroidery
uttar-pradesh_unnao.success_story_detail: Master of zari work featured preparing stencils for zari embroidery
uttar-pradesh_unnao.raw_materials: Silver and golden thread, beads, spangles, sequins, gold and silver bullion threads, kerosene oil, gateway paper
uttar-pradesh_unnao.key_facts: Zari zardozi came to India from Central Asia in the 12th century | Traditionally used on brocades, coats, velvets, ceremonial events during Mughal period | Earlier artisans earned ₹400-500 per day, reduced to ₹250 per day | A common facility centre planned under ODOP scheme to train artisans with new technologies | Fabric is fixed on wooden frame called 'adda' | Hook-tipped awl called 'ari' is commonly used tool | Regional variations exist in stitches and types of metallic threads between zardozi clusters
uttar-pradesh_unnao._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

uttarakhand_almora.success_story_detail: Local confectioner who popularized bal mithai in late 19th or early 20th century in Almora by modifying an earlier Nepalese version using milk from nearby Phalsima village
uttarakhand_almora.raw_materials: khoya (roasted milk solids), pure desi ghee, sugar, sugar balls, cardamom powder
uttarakhand_almora.key_facts: Bal mithai is approximately 1-2 inches in diameter | Contains approximately 449 calories per 100 grams | Contains about 14 grams of protein, 50 grams of carbohydrates, and 22 grams of total fat per 100 grams | Basic ingredients include approximately 1 kg of khoya, 400 g of sugar, and 100 g of sugar balls | District Almora area is 3139.00 sq km with population of 6,22,506 | Bal mithai was carried by Kumaoni soldiers during World War II as a morale booster | Almora Mithai delivers within 3 to 5 days
uttarakhand_almora._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

uttarakhand_dehradun.success_story_detail: Started in 1953 by Shri Krishan Lal Gulati, an ex-Army man. Third generation (Drona Gulati and Mudit Gulati) now running the business. Located on Rajpur Road, Dehradun.
uttarakhand_dehradun.key_facts: Elloras Bakery established in 1953 | Founder was an ex-Indian Army officer with over a decade of service | Bakery was started by British-trained chefs and butlers left behind after 1947 Independence | No-preservative policy maintained | Products expanding into Uttarakhand and northern India | Located on Rajpur Road, Dehradun

uttarakhand_haridwar.key_facts: India produces more than 70% of the world's jaggery | Jaggery is made by evaporating freshly pressed sugarcane juice and cooling the thickened liquid in moulds | Jaggery's unrefined nature means trace minerals and molasses residue are retained | Jaggery price in Pune Wholesale Mandi as of 20 Apr 2026: Maximum ₹4,325, Average ₹4,263, Minimum ₹4,201 per unit | Jaggery is also called gud in Pakistan

uttarakhand_nainital.success_story_detail: 22-year-old from Nainital, started Minakrti: The Aipan Project in December 2019, delivered over 1000 orders in one year, earns around Rs 30,000-Rs 35,000 per month, has a team of 15 members including students and village women
uttarakhand_nainital.key_facts: Aipan is a folk design art form drawn by women in red and white colour outside homes on auspicious occasions like weddings, house-warming, festivals | Each occasion or event has a different form of design in Aipan | Most orders are from people living in Delhi or other metropolitan cities who have migrated from villages | Minakshi Khati has showcased artwork at national level tourism fairs, including Uttarakhand Tourism Festival in New Delhi

uttarakhand_pithoragarh.key_facts: Pithoragarh district is the easternmost Himalayan district in Uttarakhand | District area: 7090 sq km | District population: 483439 | Literacy rate: 82.25% | 8 blocks in district | 1657 villages | 5 municipalities | 16 police stations

uttarakhand_udham-singh-nagar.cluster_names: Deshi Deoria, Bhatni, Sonughat villages, Behra Dabar, Purvanchal
uttarakhand_udham-singh-nagar.product_varieties: durries, mats, baskets, ropes, bags, decorative items, wall hangings, lampshades, torans, idols, bottle covers, containers, coasters, gifting boxes, laundry baskets, hand fans, fruit baskets
uttarakhand_udham-singh-nagar.success_story_detail: A collective initiative of 50 women from three villages (Deshi Deoria, Bhatni, Sonughat) in Uttar Pradesh organized under this name, supported by Jagriti Enterprise Center, Purvanchal
uttarakhand_udham-singh-nagar.raw_materials: Moonj grass (Saccharum munja), Kaasa grass, natural and powdered dyes (red, green, purple, yellow, pink, blue, orange)
uttarakhand_udham-singh-nagar.key_facts: Moonj grass is harvested in October and November | Dried grass is made into knots called 'Balla' locally | Grass is soaked in cold water to improve flexibility | Traditional coiling technique is employed to create products | Knowledge is passed from older women to new learners | Behra Dabar village is home to 20 artisans who gather in Kiran's courtyard | The craft is largely carried out as a secondary occupation to agriculture in Uttar Pradesh | Moonj grass thrives in subtropical climates and is found in arid and semi-arid environments

west-bengal_birbhum.product_varieties: coin bags, chappal, handbags, pouches, jewelry boxes, pencil boxes, eyeglass covers, bags, ladies' bags, piggy banks, cushion covers, sandals, wallets
west-bengal_birbhum.scheme_names: Rural Development Programme of Visva-Bharati University
west-bengal_birbhum.raw_materials: vegetable tanned leather, sheepskin, goatskin, E.I. Leather (East India Leather), paper board, cotton, velvet, silk, foam rubber, natural dyes, spirit, rubber solution, Epsom salt
west-bengal_birbhum.gi_info: Listed as 'Santiniketan Leather Goods' under the GI Act 1999 of the Government of India in July 2007
west-bengal_birbhum.key_facts: Production started as a cottage industry about 80 years ago | Craftsmen trained under Rural Development Programme of Visva-Bharati University | Material is vegetable tanned leather with artwork done by touch dyeing | Three leather types defined: 'Paper' (coarse grain), 'Bind' and 'Kid' (superior quality from smaller animals) | Vegetable tanned skins permanently retain embossed imprint of motifs or Batiks | Products are popular in foreign markets and exported to many countries
west-bengal_birbhum._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

west-bengal_cooch-behar.product_varieties: Sitalpati Shopping Bag, Tiffin Carrier Bag, Designer Hand Bag, Bottle Bag
west-bengal_cooch-behar.success_story_detail: Sitalpati mat-weaver from Ghugumari who describes the production process: each reed is slashed into 14-15 strips, then each strip is split into a further three strips
west-bengal_cooch-behar.raw_materials: Mutra cane (grown in Cooch Behar), green cane slips of Maranta dichotoma
west-bengal_cooch-behar.key_facts: Sitalpati mats are made from green cane slips split into very fine slices | The raw material mutra cane is cut all year round from marshy and swampy spots | Cane is split with a dao (sickle-like instrument with long blade about a foot long) | Green cane slips are washed in soda, dried, and soaked in water for several hours, often for 24 hours | The topmost strip among three is the coolest and used for sitalpati mats | Sitalpati mats are woven dominantly by members of the kayastha caste in Cooch Behar | Sitalpati mat is famous for the feeling of cool it imparts to the person sitting or sleeping on it
west-bengal_cooch-behar._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article|Research article

west-bengal_darjeeling.export_countries: Europe, North America, United States, United Kingdom, Japan, South Korea
west-bengal_darjeeling.export_value: 10.5 million kg in 2023 | 40% of production shipped overseas | 15% growth in 2023
west-bengal_darjeeling.key_facts: Market size USD 0.5 billion in 2024, projected USD 0.9 billion by 2034 at 6.10% CAGR | Market size USD 1.2 billion in 2025, projected USD 1.8 billion by 2033 at 5.0% CAGR | Loose Leaf segment held 55% market share in 2024 | Tea Bags segment projected to grow at 7.5% CAGR | North America holds 35% of global market in 2024 | Europe projected fastest-growing region at 7.0% CAGR | Darjeeling tea accounts for approximately 1% of total Indian tea production | Base black teas fetch around $5 per kilogram, authentic Darjeeling commands $30 to over $100 per kilogram | About 40% of Darjeeling exports are organic | Climate change projections show yield reductions exceeding 10% by 2050 | Labor shortfalls up to around 30% during peak harvest

west-bengal_hooghly.product_varieties: Bengal Tant Saree, Tangail Saree, Bengal Handloom Saree, Handloom Saree, Handloom Boutique Saree, Handloom Jamdani Tant Saree, Handloom Kotki Saree, Handloom Silk Saree, Handloom Tussar Saree
west-bengal_hooghly.success_story_detail: Owner of Dhaniakhali Saree Museum established in 1981 (or 1949 per another source), manufacturer and supplier of handloom sarees
west-bengal_hooghly.export_value: Below Rs. 0.5 Crore Approx. annual turnover (Dhaniakhali Saree Museum)
west-bengal_hooghly.raw_materials: 100-count cotton yarn, silk or zari threads
west-bengal_hooghly.gi_info: Dhaniakhali saree bears Geographic Indication (GI) tag; weaving tradition dates back to 1935
west-bengal_hooghly.key_facts: Signature design features thick dobby borders (Maathapaar or Beluaaripaar) measuring 1.5 to 2 inches in width | Originally woven in natural Kora (off-white/grey) with red or black borders | Khejur Chori technique uses dual-colored weft yarns in twisted manner | Vertical braided line near anchal/pallu distinguishes genuine Dhaniakhali sarees | Weavers transitioned from creating superfine cotton dhotis to crafting sarees

west-bengal_howrah.key_facts: Howrah district area is 1467 sq km | Population is 4,850,029 | Literacy rate is 83.31% | Howrah is the second smallest district after Kolkata | India's jewelry market projected to reach $145 billion by FY28 | India's jewelry market grew from $50 billion in 2018 to $80 billion in FY24 | Projected jewelry market CAGR is 15-16 percent | Unorganized sector accounts for 62-64 percent of retail jewelry market | Over 500,000 local goldsmiths and jewelers exist in India | India's gold consumption is 66 percent jewelry and 34 percent bars and coins | West Bengal is among top 5 states for organized retail jewelry stores | Government implemented mandatory hallmarking for gold purity assurance

west-bengal_kolkata.success_story_detail: Founded in 1981, has over 850 retail stores across India, expanded internationally with Khadim Shoe Bangladesh Limited in 2019, manufacturing units in Kolkata and Kanpur
west-bengal_kolkata.key_facts: Khadim India founded in 1981 | ISO 9001:2000 and ISO 9001:2008 certified | Khadim has two manufacturing units in Kolkata and Kanpur | Over 850 retail stores across India | Khadim Shoe Bangladesh Limited established in 2019 | Acknit Industries founded in 1990 | Farinni Leather founded in 1988 | Lexus Exports founded 2000+ | Param Leathers founded in 2018 | Excelsior Leathers founded in 1989 | XL Enterprises Ltd founded in 1991 | Kolkata has rich heritage of leather craftsmanship passed down for generations | Council for Leather Exports supports the industry

west-bengal_malda.key_facts: Malda District covers an area of 3733 sq km | Malda shares a 165.5 km international border with Bangladesh | It takes around 2,500 silkworms to produce a pound of raw silk | A single cocoon has somewhere around a thousand yards of silk filaments | India produces 28,708 metric tons of silk annually (second globally after China at 146,000 metric tons)

west-bengal_murshidabad.product_varieties: Pure Silk Sarees, Block Printed Sarees, Hand Painted Sarees

west-bengal_murshidabad.key_facts: Murshidabad is located on the banks of the Bhagirathi River in West Bengal | Murshidabad silk production dates back to the Mughal era | Murshidabad silk is known for its lightweight, soft, and lustrous texture | Silk Mark certification guarantees authenticity and purity of silk used in sarees | Block printing and hand-painting are traditional techniques used on Murshidabad silk sarees | Designs include floral patterns, paisleys, geometric shapes, and motifs from nature, mythology, and Indian epics

west-bengal_paschim-medinipur-west-medin.raw_materials: cloth, paper, clay, soot (for black), raktachandan tree seeds (for red), turmeric (for yellow)
west-bengal_paschim-medinipur-west-medin.key_facts: Patachitra is a traditional scroll painting folk art form | The word 'pata' is derived from Sanskrit word 'patta' meaning cloth | Painters are called Potuas | Potuas sing songs called 'pater gan' as they unfurl and display paintings | Naya village is located in Pingla block of West Medinipur | Patachitra paintings tell stories from Hindu epics like Ramayana and Mahabharata, as well as contemporary themes like environmental conservation and social justice | Potuas use natural colors from locally available sources | The art form has been passed down through generations as a family trade | Both men and women actively engage in the art today

west-bengal_purba-bardhaman.key_facts: Purba Bardhaman district area: 5433 sq km | Population (2011 Census): 4,835,532 | Literacy Rate: 75.48% | Blocks: 23 | Subdivisions: 4 | Municipalities: 6 | District known as 'The Rice Bowl of Bengal'

west-bengal_purba-medinipur-east-medinip.key_facts: 8 species of palaemonid prawns were identified in Purba Medinipur | Survey conducted between February and September 2019 | Macrobrachium hendersodayanum and M. tiwarii are newly recorded in West Bengal | M. javanicum reported for first time from the district | Total number of palaemonid species in Purba Medinipur stands to 18 | Nematopalaemon tenuipes appears locally extinct, last recorded in 1969 | Prawns collected from rivers, ponds, and canals using multiple net types

west-bengal_purulia.product_varieties: varnishes, jewellery, cable insulation coating, toys, sealing wax
west-bengal_purulia.success_story_detail: Started lac cultivation in 1992 on 5 Kusum and 250 Palas trees; experienced initial losses; after KVK guidance in 1993, implemented scientific lac cultivation techniques; presently cultivating on 2500 Palas trees, 19 Kusum trees, and 32 Ber trees; became a master trainer
west-bengal_purulia.key_facts: Lac cultivation is the oldest and most visible economic activity in Purulia district since time immemorial | A large part of total lac production in West Bengal comes from Purulia district | 12% of Purulia's total geographical area (approx 751 sq. km.) is under forest | Lac cultivation involves simple operations: climbing trees for pruning, tying brood lac bundles, harvesting, and spraying pesticides (male labour) and bundling, selection of broodlac, collection and scraping (female labour) | Data on lac production from 1982-83 to 2011-12 showed declining trend and negative growth rate | Mean maximum temperature, particularly in summer season, has negative relationship with lac production
west-bengal_purulia._source: Official Govt ODOP page|Research article|Research article

west-bengal_south-24-parganas.product_varieties: art silk sarees, cotton sarees, handloom sarees, tant sarees, bangalore silk kantha saree, Designer Saree, Designer Fancy Saree
west-bengal_south-24-parganas.success_story_detail: Supplier and wholesaler located in Barasat, South-24-parganas, West Bengal with annual turnover Upto Rs. 10 Lakh
west-bengal_south-24-parganas.key_facts: Company Annual Turnover: Upto Rs. 10 Lakh | Legal Status: Proprietorship | Estimated Delivery Time: 5 To 7 Days | Payment Terms: 7 to 30 days after invoice date | Return Policy: Easy 7 days return, refund and exchange

west-bengal_uttar-dinajpur-north-dinajpu.key_facts: Tulaipanji name derived from 'tulai' (light/delicate) and 'panji' (rice) | Grains retain taste for around a year or more | Used in Bengali dishes like pulao, payesh, biryani, pitha and payas | Medium-slender white kernels with pleasant aroma | Known for pest resistance | Cooking time: 8-10 minutes | Designated as non-Basmati aromatic rice variety | Sourced from Northern province (Uttar Dinajpur) in West Bengal
